CHAPTER VIII. Bildad showeth God's justice. PJVHEN answered Bildad: the: Shuhite, and said,
That they might set their hope in God, and not forget the works of God, but keep his commandments:
Who by him do believe in God, that raised him up from the dead, and gave him glory; that your faith and hope might be in God.
Blessed [be] the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, which according to his abundant mercy hath begotten us again unto a lively hope by the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead,
But sanctify the Lord God in your hearts: and [be] ready always to [give] an answer to every man that asketh you a reason of the hope that is in you with meekness and fear:
Remembering without ceasing your work of faith, and labour of love, and patience of hope in our Lord Jesus Christ, in the sight of God and our Father;
PAUL, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the commandment of God our Saviour, and Lord Jesus Christ, [which is] our hope;
And it shall be, if thou do at all forget the LORD thy God, and walk after other gods, and serve them, and worship them, I testify against you this day that ye shall surely perish.
For the preaching of the cross is to them that perish foolishness; but unto us which are saved it is the power of God.

And I said unto them that it was the word of God; and whoso would hearken unto the word of God, and would hold fast unto it, they would never perish; neither could the temptations and the fiery darts…
For we are unto God a sweet savour of Christ, in them that are saved, and in them that perish:
And he that fighteth against Zion shall perish, saith God.
Wherefore, because of my blessing the Lord God will not suffer that ye shall perish; wherefore, he will be merciful unto you and unto your seed forever.
